CuNH322++NH3⇌K3CuNH332+

CuNH332++NH3⇌K4CuNH342+

questions.solution:
Cu2++NH3⇌K1CuNH32+

CuNH32++NH3⇌K2CuNH322+

CuNH322++NH3⇌K3CuNH332+

CuNH332++NH3⇌K4CuNH342+

Cu2++4NH3⇌KCuNH342+

So,

K=K1×K2×K3×K4

=104×1.58×103×5×102×102

K=7.9×1011

Where K→ Equilibrium constant for formation of CuNH342+
So equilibrium constant K' for dissociation
 of CuNH342+ is 1 K

K'=1 K

K'=17.9×1011

=1.26×10-12=x×10-12

So the value of x=1.26

OMR Ans =1 (After rounded off to the nearest integer)

questions.q:
The number of correct statements from the following is ___________

(A) Conductivity always decreases with decrease in concentration for both strong and weak electrolytes.

(B) The number of ions per unit volume that carry current in a solution increases on dilution.

(C) Molar conductivity increases with decrease in concentration.

(D) The variation in molar conductivity is different for strong and weak electrolytes.

(E) For weak electrolytes, the change in molar conductivity with dilution is due to decrease in degree of dissociation.
questions.solution:
Conductivity always decreases with the decrease in concentration both, for weak and strong electrolytes.

This can be explained by the fact that the number of ions per unit volume that carry the current in a solution decreases on dilution.

Molar conductivity of a strong and weak electrolyte increases with dilution. On dilution as volume of solution increases. Thus, on dilution, ions get more apart and mobility of ions increases which leads to increase in molar conductivity of the solution.

Except B and E, all statements are correct.
questions.source: Top 100 Numerical Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

Sn2++2e-→Sn

Sn4++4e-→Sn the electrode potentials are ; ESn2+/Sn°=-0.140 V and ESn4+/Sn°=0.010 V. The magnitude of standard electrode potential for Sn4+/Sn2+ i.e. ESn4+/Sn2+° is____×10-2 V(Nearest integer)
questions.solution:
(i) Sn2++2e-⟶Sn         E10=-0.14 V

ΔG10=-2 F-0.14

(ii) Sn4++4e-⟶Sn       E20=+0.010 VΔG20=-4F+0.010

Target Sn4++2e-⟶Sn2+      E30=?

ΔG30=-2 FE30

Target Eq.=Eq. ii - Eq. i

-2FE30=-4F0.010--2F-0.14

E30=4×0.010+2×0.142=0.16 V=16×10-2 V

questions.q: At <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>310</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>, the solubility of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CaF</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> in water is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>34</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>/</mo><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math>. The solubility product of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CaF</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is ---- <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>mol</mi><mo>/</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>3</mn></msup></math>(nearest integer). (Given molar mass : <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CaF</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>78</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>mol</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
The solubility product constant is the equilibrium constant for the dissolution of a solid substance into an aqueous solution. It is denoted by the symbol Ksp.

Solubility of CaF2=S mole//L

S=2.34×10-30.1×78=2.3478×10-2=3×10-4 mol/L

KspCaF2=4 S3=43×10-43

=108×10-12

=0.0108×10-8mol/L3

When <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi><mo> </mo><mi>of</mi><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo> </mo><msub><mi>CH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mi>COONa</mi></math> solution is mixed with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi><mo> </mo><mi>of</mi><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>02</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo> </mo><msub><mi>CH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mi>COOH</mi></math> solution, the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>pH</mi></math> of the resultant solution is found to be equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> is _______.

The Henderson equation for the given acidic buffer can be written as 

pH=pKa+logsaltweak acid

5=pKa+log25×0.225×0.02⇒pKa=4⇒Ka=10-4=10×10-5

Hence, the value of x is 10.

Now using

so v=2K.E.m

=6.637.6×10-3410-25=66.3×10-10 m7.6

=8.72×10-10 m

≈9×10-10 m

=9 Ao

questions.solution:
n=5, T=293 K=const,ΔU=0

P1=2.1 MPa,P2=1.3 MPa

Pext=4.3 MPa=const.

W=-PextV2-V1=-PextnRTP2-nRTP1

=-4.3×5×8.314×29311.3-12.1

=-4.3×5×8.314×2932.1-1.31.3×2.1

=-15347.7 J

or, W=-15.35 kJ

ΔU0=q+W

∴  q=-W

or, q=15.35 kJ (for 5 moles)

∴  q/mole=15.355=3 kJ mol-1

The magnitude enthalpy of formation of propane <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>8</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>kJmol</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. (Nearest integer)

The combustion reaction can be written as follows,

C3H8g+5O2→3CO2+4H2O; ∆H=-2220.0 kJ mol-1

Cgraphite+O2g→CO2g ; ∆H=-393.5 kJ mol-1

3Cgraphite+4H2g→C3H8 g∆Hf=3∆HC°graphite+4∆HC°H2g-∆HC°propane∆Hf=3×-393.5+4×-285.8--2220=-103.7 kJ mol-1

questions.q: A gas ( Molar mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>=</mo><mn>280</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>mol</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>) was burnt in excess <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> in a constant volume calorimeter and during combustion the temperature of calorimeter increased from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>298</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>298</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>45</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>. If the heat capacity of calorimeter is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kJ</mi><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> and enthalpy of combustion of gas is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kJ</mi><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>mol</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> then amount of gas burnt is____<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
Let x g is burnt

Moles=x280 heat released by x280 mole=2.5×0.45 kJ heat released by 1 mole =2.5×0.45×280xkJ

ΔH=ΔU+ΔngRT

ΔH≃ΔU

9=2.5×280×0.45x

x=35 g

questions.q: When <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> moles of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>He</mi></math> gas expand isothermally and reversibly at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> litre to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn></math> litre, the magnitude of the maximum work obtained is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>. [nearest integer ] (Given : <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>mol</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>log</mi><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>3010</mn></math>)
questions.solution:
Work done in isothermal reversible process is calculated by the equation

W=-2.303nRTlogV2V1n=Number of molesT=Temperature=300KV2=Final volume=20 litresV1=Initial volume=10 litres   

W=-2.303×5×8.3×300log2010

=-8630.38 J

questions.q:
When 600 mL of 0.2M HNO3 is mixed with 400 mL of 0.1M NaOH solution in a flask, the rise in temperature of the flask is_____×10-2 °C
(Enthalpy of neutralisation =57 kJ mol-1 and Specific heat of water =4.2JK-1 g-1) (Neglect heat capacity of flask)
questions.solution:
Number of moles of given acid and base are

HNO3 = 6001000×0.2 M= 0.12 moles NaOH = 4001000×0.1 M = 0.04 moles

Now neutralisation reaction between them will be

HNO3    +  NaOH  →  NaNO3+H2O0.12 mole  0.04 mole0.08 mole   0 mole      0.04 mole

Heat relesed during this reaction will be 

ΔrH=0.04×57×103 J 

=2280 J

Now temperature increased can be calculated by

mSΔT=2280 J

⇒1000 mL×1gmmL×4.2×ΔT=2280

ΔT=22804.2×10-3

=542.86×10-3

ΔT=54.286×10-2  °C

questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>17</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> completely vapourises at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>-</mo><mn>33</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>42</mn><mo> </mo><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn></math> bar pressure and the enthalpy change in the process is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>23</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kJ</mi><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>mol</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The enthalpy change for the vapourisation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>85</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> under the same conditions is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>kJ</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
17.0 g of NH3 = 1717 = 1 mole and the enthalpy change = 23.4 kJ mol-1.

 85 g of NH3 = 8517 = 5 moles enthalpy change under the same conditions will be = 23.4×5=117 kJ.

questions.q:
28.0 L of CO2 is produced on complete combustion of 16.8 L gaseous mixture of ethene and methane at 25°C and 1 atm. Heat evolved during the combustion process is kJ

Given:ΔHCCH4=-900 kJ mol-1

ΔHCC2H4=-1400 kJ mol-1.
questions.solution:
Complete combustion of ethene and methane mixture produces carbon dioxide.
Let, Volume of C2H4 is x litre

C2H4+3O2→2CO2+2H2O

Initial volume of carbon dioxide 0
Final volume of carbon dioxide 2x

CH4+2O2→CO2+2H2O

Initial volume of carbon dioxide is 0

Final volume of carbon dioxide is (16.8-x)

Total volume of CO2=2x+16.8-x

⇒28=16.8+x

x=11.2 L

nCH4=PVRT=1×5.60.082×298=0.229 mole

nC2H4=11.20.082×298=0.458 mole

∴ Heat evolved using standard enthalpy of combustion =0.229×900+0.458×1400

=206.1+641.2

=847.3 kJ
